methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.5B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $23B

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Aeva Technologies is a publicly traded semiconductor company founded by Soroush Salehian. The target's peer universe comprises 3 public and 8 private comparable companies in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including public peers Cerebras, Credo Semiconductor, and Ouster, as well as private frontier companies like PsiQuantum and Etched.

The peer-median method is applicable and anchors valuation to three same-sector, same-stage public comparables. Across this set, median valuation is $5.0B, with a range spanning $1.5B (Ouster) to $23.0B (Cerebras). This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in market positioning and maturity within the semiconductor peer group. A public-comp anchoring approach using price-to-sales multiples is not applicable here; the sector median P/S across three public tickers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) is 25.3x, but Aeva's revenue is not on file, precluding this triangulation method.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $4.2B to $5.8B, with a median of $5.0B, derived solely from peer-median positioning. Key uncertainties include Aeva's revenue trajectory and competitive standing relative to Cerebras and Credo—the two higher-valued peers in the comparable set. The wide peer range ($1.5B–$23.0B) reflects material dispersion in how the market values semiconductor frontier companies at this stage. Without revenue disclosure or forward-looking unit economics, the peer-median method provides the only quantitative anchor available.
